Annual Exam - 2020

Code: 65/1/1 (set-1)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  2. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  3. Please check that this question paper contains 36 questions.
  4. Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answerbook during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper comprises four Sections A, B, C and D. This question paper carries 36 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A - Questions no. 1 to 20 comprises of 20 questions of 1 mark each.
  3. Section B - Questions no. 21 to 26 comprises of 6 questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section C - Questions no. 27 to 32 comprises of 6 questions of 4 marks each.
  5. Section D - Questions no. 33 to 36 comprises of 4 questions of 6 marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice in the question paper. However, an internal choice has been provided in 3 questions of one mark, 2 questions of two marks, 2 questions of four marks and 2 questions of six marks. Only one of the choices in such questions have to be attempted.
  7. In addition to this, separate instructions are given with each section and question, wherever necessary.
  8. Use of calculators is not permitted.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 20 carry 1 mark each.
Question numbers 1 to 10 are multiple choice type questions. Select the correct option
1.

If A is a square matrix of order 3 and |A| = 5, then the value of |2A'| is:

a. -10
b. 10
c. -40
d. 40
2.

If A is a square matrix such that A2 = A, then (I - A)3 + A is equal to:

a. I
b. 0
c. I - A
d. I + A
3.

The principal value of {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{3 \pi}{5}\right){/tex} is

a. {tex}\frac{2 \pi}{5}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{-2 \pi}{5}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{3 \pi}{5}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{-3 \pi}{5}{/tex}
4.

If the projection of {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} on {tex}\vec{\text{b}}=2 \hat{\text{i}}+\lambda \hat{\text{k}}{/tex} is zero, then the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} is:

a. 0
b. 1
c. {tex}\frac{-2}{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{-3}{2}{/tex}
5.

The vector equation of the line passing through the point (-1, 5, 4) and perpendicular to the plane z = 0 is:

a. {tex}\vec{r}=-\hat{i}+5 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}+\lambda(\hat{i}+\hat{j}){/tex}
b. {tex}\vec{r}=-\hat{i}+5 \hat{j}+(4+\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex}
c. {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}+\lambda \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}
d. {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda \hat{k}{/tex}
6.

The number of arbitrary constants in the particular solution of a differential equation of second order is (are):

a. 0
b. 1
c. 2
d. 3
7.

{tex}\int\limits_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex}sec2 x is equal to

a. -1
b. 0
c. 1
d. 2
8.

The length of the perpendicular drawn from the point (4, -7, 3) on the y-axis is:

a. 3 units
b. 4 units
c. 5 units
d. 7 units
9.

If A and B are two independent events with P(A) = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and P(B) = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}, then P(B'|A) is equal to:

a. {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
d. 1
10.

The corner points of the feasible region determined by the system of linear inequalities are (0, 0), (4, 0), (2, 4), and (0, 5). If the maximum value of z = ax + by, where a, b > 0 occurs at both (2, 4) and (4, 0), then:

a. a = 2b
b. 2a = b
c. a = b
d. 3a = b

Fill in the blanks in question numbers 11 to 15.
11.

A relation R in a set A is called ________, if (a1, a2) {tex}\in{/tex} R implies (a2, a1) {tex}\in{/tex} R, for all a1, a2 {tex}\in{/tex} A.
12.

The greatest integer function defined by f(x) = [x], 0 < x < 2 is not differentiable at x = ________.
13.

If A is a matrix of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2, then the order of the matrix A′ is ________.

OR

A square matrix A is said to be skew-symmetric, if ________.
14.

The equation of the normal to the curve y2 = 8x at the origin is ________.

OR

The radius of a circle is increasing at the uniform rate of 3 cm/sec. At the instant when the radius of the circle is 2 cm, its area increases at the rate of ________ cm2/s.
15.

The position vectors of two points A and B are {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{OA}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{OB}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} respectively. The position vector of a point P which divides the line segment joining A and B in the ratio 2 : 1 is ________.

Question numbers 16 to 20 are very short answer type questions.
16.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 0 \\ -1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 3 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find A(adj A).
17.

Find: {tex}\int {/tex}x4 log x dx

OR

Find: {tex}\int \frac{2 \text x}{\sqrt[3]{\text x^2+1}} \text {dx}{/tex}
18.

Evaluate: {tex}\int_\limits{1}^{3}{/tex} |2x - 1| dx
19.

Two cards are drawn at random and one-by-one without replacement from a well-shuffled pack of 52 playing cards. Find the probability that one card is red and the other is black.
20.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{9-4 x^{2}}}{/tex}

SECTION B

Question numbers 21 to 26 carry 2 marks each.
21.

Prove that {tex}\sin ^{-1}(2 x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}=2 \cos ^{-1} x{/tex}, {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\le{/tex} x {tex}\le{/tex} 1.

OR

Consider a bijective function f : R+ {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} (7, {tex}\infty{/tex}) given by f(x) = 16x2 + 24x + 7, where R+ is the set of all positive real numbers. Find the inverse function of f.
22.

If x = at2, y = 2at, then find {tex}\frac{\text d^{2} \text y}{\text {dx}^{2}}{/tex}.
23.

Find the points on the curve y = x3 - 3x2 - 4x at which the tangent lines are parallel to the line 4x + y - 3 = 0.
24.

Find a unit vector perpendicular to each of the vectors {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} where {tex}\vec{a}=5 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=7 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+6 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}.

OR

Find the volume of the parallelopiped whose adjacent edges are represented by {tex}2 \vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex}, {tex}-\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} and {tex}3 \vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex}, where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}, and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}.
25.

Find the value of k so that the lines x = - y = kz and x - 2 = 2y + 1 = - z + 1 are perpendicular to each other.
26.

The probability of finding a green signal on a busy crossing X is 30%. What is the probability of finding a green signal on X on two consecutive days out of three?

SECTION C

Question numbers 27 to 32 carry 4 marks each.
27.

Let N be the set of natural numbers and R be the relation on N{tex}\times{/tex}N defined by (a, b) R (c, d) iff ad = bc for all a, b, c, d {tex}\in{/tex} N. Show that R is an equivalence relation.
28.

If y = {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}^2 \cos \mathrm{x}}{/tex} + (cos x)x, then find {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}.
29.

Find: {tex}\int{/tex}sec3 x dx
30.

Find the general solution of the differential equation y ey dx = (y3 + 2xey) dy.

OR

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}y-x \tan \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}, given that {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} at x = 1
31.

A furniture trader deals in only two items - chairs and tables. He has ₹ 50,000 to invest and a space to store at most 35 items. A chair costs him ₹ 1,000 and a table costs him ₹ 2,000. The trader earns a profit of ₹ 150 and ₹ 250 on a chair and table, respectively. Formulate the above problem as an LPP to maximize the profit and solve it graphically.
32.

There are two bags, I and II, Bag I contains 3 red and 5 black balls and Bag II contains 4 red and 3 black balls. One ball is transferred randomly from Bag I to Bag II and then a ball is drawn randomly from Bag II. If the ball so drawn is found to be black in colour, then find the probability that the transferred ball is also black.

OR

An urn contains 5 red, 2 white and 3 black balls. Three balls are drawn, one-by-one, at random without replacement. Find the probability distribution of the number of white balls. Also, find the mean and the variance of the number of white balls drawn.

SECTION D

Question numbers 33 to 36 carry 6 marks each.
33.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & -2 \\ 2 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find A-1 and use it to solve the following system of the equations:
x + 2y - 3z = 6
3x + 2y - 2z = 3
2x - y + z = 2

OR

Using properties of determinants, prove that {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} (b+c)^{2} & a^{2} & b c \\ (c+a)^{2} & b^{2} & c a \\ (a+b)^{2} & c^{2} & a b \end{array}\right|{/tex} = (a – b) (b – c) (c – a) (a + b + c) (a2 + b2 + c2).
34.

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the triangle whose vertices are (2, -2), (4, 5) and (6, 2).
35.

Show that the height of the right circular cylinder of greatest volume which can be inscribed in a right circular cone of height h and radius r is one-third of the height of the cone, and the greatest volume of the cylinder is {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} times the volume of the cone.
36.

Find the equation of the plane that contains the point A(2, 1, -1) and is perpendicular to the line of intersection of the planes 2x + y - z = 3 and x + 2y + z = 2. Also, find the angle between the plane thus obtained and the y-axis.

OR

Find the distance of the point P(-2, - 4, 7) from the point of intersection Q of the line {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+6 \hat{\mathrm{k}})+\lambda(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} and the plane {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k})=6{/tex}. Also, write the vector equation of the line PQ.

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper comprises four Sections A, B, C and D. This question paper carries 36 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A - Questions no. 1 to 20 comprises of 20 questions of 1 mark each.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 20 carry 1 mark each.
Question numbers 1 to 10 are multiple choice type questions. Select the correct option
1.

If A is a square matrix of order 3 and |A| = 5, then the value of |2A'| is:

a. -10
b. 10
c. -40
d. 40
Correct Answer: (d) 40
Explanation:

40

 1
2.

If A is a square matrix such that A2 = A, then (I - A)3 + A is equal to:

a. I
b. 0
c. I - A
d. I + A
Correct Answer: (a) I
Explanation:

Given that A2 = A
Calculating value of (I - A)3 + A:
(I - A)3 + A = I3 - 3I2A + 3IA2 - A3 +A
= I - A2A - 3A + 3A2 + A ({tex}\therefore{/tex} In = I and IA = A) 
= I - AA - 3A + 3A + A ({tex}\therefore{/tex} A2 = A) 
= I + A2 - 3A + 3A + A 
= I
Hence, (I - A)3 + A = {tex}I{/tex}

3.

The principal value of {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{3 \pi}{5}\right){/tex} is

a. {tex}\frac{2 \pi}{5}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{-2 \pi}{5}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{3 \pi}{5}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{-3 \pi}{5}{/tex}
Correct Answer: (b) {tex}\frac{-2 \pi}{5}{/tex}
Explanation:

{tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{3 \pi}{5}\right){/tex}
This can be written as:
{tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{3 \pi}{5}\right) {/tex}
{tex}= \tan^1(\tan[\pi-\frac {2\pi}5]){/tex}
= {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(-\tan \frac{2 \pi}{5}\right){/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} tan ({tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = -tan x)
= {tex}-\tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{2 \pi}{5}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{-2 \pi}{5}{/tex}

    23.

    Find the points on the curve y = x3 - 3x2 - 4x at which the tangent lines are parallel to the line 4x + y - 3 = 0.

    Solution:

    First, we will find the slope of tangent,
    {tex} y=x^3-3 x^2-4 x {/tex}
    {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} x^3-\frac{d}{d x} 3 x^2-\frac{d}{d x} 4 x {/tex}
    {tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=3 x^2-3 \cdot 2 x^{2-1}-4 x^{1-1}=3 x^2-6 x-4 {/tex} ...(1)
    Now the line {tex}4 x+y-3=0 \Rightarrow y=-4 x+3{/tex}
    {tex}y=-4 x+3{/tex} is the form of equation of a straight-line {tex}y=m x+c{/tex}, here {tex}m{/tex} is the slope of the line.
    Hence the slope {tex}{m}=-4=\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}
    Substitute this in equation (1)
    {tex} \therefore-4= 3 x^2-6 x-4 \Rightarrow 3 x^2-6 x=0 \Rightarrow 3 x(x-2)=0 {/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow 3 x=0 \text { or } x-2=0 \Rightarrow x=0 \text { or } x=2 {/tex}
    For {tex}{x}=0{/tex} substitute it in {tex}{y}={x}^3-3 {x}^2-4 {x}{/tex}
    {tex} \therefore y=(0)^3-3(0)^2-4(0)=0{/tex}
    Hence point is {tex}(0,0){/tex}
    For {tex}{x}=2{/tex} substitute it in {tex}{y}={x}^3-3 {x}^2-4 {x}{/tex}
    {tex} \therefore y=(2)^3-3(2)^2-4(2)=8-12-8=-12{/tex}
    Hence point is {tex}(2,-12){/tex}
    Therefore, points on the curve are (0, 0) and (2.-12)

    The probability of finding a green signal on a busy crossing X is 30%. What is the probability of finding a green signal on X on two consecutive days out of three?

    Solution:

    27.

    Let N be the set of natural numbers and R be the relation on N{tex}\times{/tex}N defined by (a, b) R (c, d) iff ad = bc for all a, b, c, d {tex}\in{/tex} N. Show that R is an equivalence relation.

    Solution:
    1. Reflexivity:
      let (a,b) {tex}\in{/tex} R, Then,
      (a,b) R (a, b) since ab = ba 
      Thus, (a,b) R (a,b) {tex}\forall(a, b) \in R{/tex}
      Thus, R is reflexive.
    2. Symmetry:
      Let (a,b) R (c,d) Then 
      (a,b) R (c,d) => ad = bc
      => bc = ad 
      => cb = da
      => (c, d) R (a,b)
      R is symmetric.
    3. Transitivity
      Let (a,b) R (c,d) and (c,d) R (e,f) Then,
      ad = bc and cf = de
      => adcf = bcde
      => {tex}(a f)(c d)=(b \varepsilon)(c d){/tex}
      => (a,b) R (e,f)
      {tex}\therefore(a, b) R(c, d) \text { and }(c, d) R(e, f) \Rightarrow(a, b) R(e, f){/tex}
      Therefore, R is transitive. Thus, R is reflexive, symmetric and transitive.
      Hence, R is an equivalence relation.

    Find the general solution of the differential equation y ey dx = (y3 + 2xey) dy.

    Solution:

    {tex} {ye}^{{y}} {dx}=\left({y}^3+2 {xe}^y\right) {dy}{/tex}
    {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{y^3+2 x^y}{y^y}{/tex} by dividing the above equation by {tex}{ye}^y{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d y}-\frac{2}{y} x=y^2 e^{-y}{/tex} by seperating the variables
    {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}-\frac{2}{y} x=y^2 e^{-y}{/tex} is of the form {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}+P x=Q{/tex}
    where {tex}P=\frac{2}{y}{/tex} and {tex}Q=y^2 e^{-y}{/tex}
    Integrating Factor {tex}=\text { I.F. }={e}^{\int {P}({x}) . {dx}}{/tex}
    Now,
    {tex} x \times I . F=\int Q \times I . F d y+c {/tex}
    {tex} \frac{x}{y^2}=\int y^2 e^{-y} \times \frac{1}{y^2} d y+c {/tex}
    {tex} =-e^{-y}+c \text { where } \int e^{-x} d x=-e^{-x}+c {/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{x}{y^2}=-e^{-y}+c {/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow x=-y^2 e^{-y}+c y^2 {/tex}

    Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}y-x \tan \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}, given that {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} at x = 1

    Solution:

    We have, {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}=y-x \tan \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}-\tan \left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}
    This is a homogeneous differential equation.
    Now, put {tex}y=v x \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}
    {tex} \therefore v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=v-\tan v {/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}=-\tan v \Rightarrow \frac{d v}{\tan v}=-\frac{d x}{x} \Rightarrow \cot v d v+\frac{d x}{x}=0 {/tex}
    Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\log |\sin v|+\log x=\log C{/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow x \sin v=C \Rightarrow x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=C{/tex}
    When {tex}x=1, y=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}, we get {tex}1 \cdot \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=C \Rightarrow C=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} So, {tex}x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} is the required particular solution.

    An urn contains 5 red, 2 white and 3 black balls. Three balls are drawn, one-by-one, at random without replacement. Find the probability distribution of the number of white balls. Also, find the mean and the variance of the number of white balls drawn.

    Solution:

    Three balls drawn, one by one, without replacement is same as drawing three balls simultaneous.
    Total number of balls = 5 + 2 + 3 = 10, 2 balls are white and 8 balls are non-white.
    Let X denote the number of white balls drawn in a random draw of three balls, then X can take values 0, 1, 2.
    P(0) = P (3 non-white balls) = {tex}\frac{{ }^{8} \mathrm{C}_{3}}{{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{3}}=\frac{8.7 .6}{1.2 .3} \times \frac{1.2 .3}{10.9 .8}=\frac{7}{15}{/tex},
    P(1) = P (1 white, 2 non-white) = {tex}\frac{{ }^{2} \mathrm{C}_{1} \times{ }^{8} \mathrm{C}_{2}}{{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{3}}=2 \times \frac{8.7}{1.2} \times \frac{1.2 \cdot 3}{10.9 .8}=\frac{7}{15}{/tex},
    P(2) = P (2 white, 1 non-white) = {tex}\frac{{ }^{2} \mathrm{C}_{2} \times{ }^{8} \mathrm{C}_{1}}{{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{3}}{/tex} = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1.2 .3}{10.9 .8}=\frac{1}{15}{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} The probability distribution of white balls is {tex}\left(\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ \frac{7}{15} & \frac{7}{15} & \frac{1}{15} \end{array}\right){/tex}
    Mean = {tex}\mu{/tex} = {tex}\sum{/tex}pixi = {tex}\frac{7}{15}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 0 + {tex}\frac{7}{15}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + {tex}\frac{1}{15}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = {tex}\frac{9}{15}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
    {tex}\sum{/tex}pi{tex}x^{2}_{i}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{15}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 02 + {tex}\frac{7}{15}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 + {tex}\frac{1}{15}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 22 = 0 + {tex}\frac{7}{15}+\frac{4}{15}=\frac{11}{15}{/tex}
    Variance = {tex}\sum{/tex}pi{tex}x^{2}_{i}{/tex} - {tex}\mu^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{11}{15}-\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^{2}=\frac{11}{15}-\frac{9}{25}=\frac{55-27}{75}=\frac{28}{75}{/tex}

    {tex}=1(2-2)-2(3+4)-3(-3-4){/tex}
    {tex}=0-14+21=7 \neq 0{/tex}.
    {tex}A^{-1}{/tex} Exist
    {tex}A_{11}=\left|\begin{array}{cc}2 & -2 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
    {tex}A_{12}=-\left|\begin{array}{cc}3 & -2 \\ 2 & 1\end{array}\right|=-(3+4)=-7{/tex}
    {tex}A_{13}=\left|\begin{array}{cc}3 & 2 \\ 2 & -1\end{array}\right|=-3-4=-7{/tex}.
    {tex}A_{21}=-\left|\begin{array}{cc}2 & -3 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right|=-(2-3) =1{/tex}
    {tex}A_{22}=\left|\begin{array}{cc}1 & -3 \\ 2 & 1\end{array}\right|=1+6=7{/tex}
    {tex}A_{23}=-\left|\begin{array}{cc}1 & 2 \\ 2 & -1\end{array}\right|=-(-1-4)=5{/tex}
    {tex} A_{31}=\left|\begin{array}{ll}2 & -3 \\ 2 & -2\end{array}\right|=-4+6=2 {/tex}
    {tex} A_{32}=-\left|\begin{array}{ll}1 & -3 \\ 3 & -2\end{array}\right|=-(-2+9)=-7{/tex}
    {tex}A_{33}=\left|\begin{array}{ll}1 & 2 \\ 3 & 2\end{array}\right|=-4{/tex}
    {tex}\operatorname{Adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & -7 & -7 \\ 1 & 7 & 5 \\ 2 & -7 & -4\end{array}\right]'{/tex}
    {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore A^{-1} =\frac{1}{A} \text { adj } A . {/tex}
    {tex}=\frac{1}{7}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex}
    Now the systeem of eq. cans be reduced in the matrix from:
    {tex}A x=B{/tex} as.
    {tex} x_0=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l}6 \\ 3 \\ 2\end{array}\right] {/tex}

    {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right] } =\frac{1}{7}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}6 \\ 3 \\ 2\end{array}\right] {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{1}{7}\left[\begin{array}{c}7 \\ -35 \\ -35\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}1 \\ -5 \\ -5\end{array}\right]{/tex}
    x = 1

    35.

    Show that the height of the right circular cylinder of greatest volume which can be inscribed in a right circular cone of height h and radius r is one-third of the height of the cone, and the greatest volume of the cylinder is {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} times the volume of the cone.

    Solution:

    The below figure represents the cylinder inscribed in cone,

    Schematic diagram
    In a cone, radius {tex}={R}{/tex}, height {tex}={H}{/tex}
    Semi vertical angle {tex}=\theta{/tex}
    In a cylinder, radius= {tex}r{/tex}, height=h
    The expression of similar triangle,
    {tex}\triangle {ACE} \sim \triangle {FCD}{/tex}
    {tex} \therefore \frac{{CE}}{{EA}}=\frac{{CD}}{{DF}}{/tex}
    Substitute the given values,
    {tex} \therefore \frac{H}{R}=\frac{H-h}{r} {/tex}
    {tex} \therefore h=H-\frac{H}{R} \gamma {/tex}
    The expression of volume of cylinder,
    {tex} {V}=\pi {r}^2 {~h}{/tex}
    Substitute the given value of height of cylinder,
    {tex} V=\pi r^2\left(H-\frac{H}{R} r\right) {/tex}
    {tex} \therefore V=\pi r^2 H-\pi r^3 \frac{H}{R} {/tex}
    Differentiate the above expression with respect to radius r and equate it to zero,
    {tex} \therefore \frac{{d}}{{dr}}\left(\pi r^2 H-\pi r^3 \frac{H}{R}\right)=0 {/tex}
    {tex} \therefore 2 \pi r H-3 \pi r^2 \frac{H}{R}=0 {/tex}
    {tex} \left.\therefore r=\frac{2 R}{3} \text { (because radius }>0\right) {/tex}
    Second differentiate at the given value of radius is negative. So, it gives maxima.
    Substitute the value of radius in the height of cylinder.
    {tex} \therefore h=H-\frac{H}{R}\left(\frac{2 R}{3}\right) {/tex}
    {tex} \therefore h=\frac{H}{3} {/tex}
    Substitute the calculated value of radius and height in the expression of volume of cylinder.
    {tex} \therefore {V}_{\max }=\pi\left(\frac{2 {R}}{3}\right)^2\left(\frac{{H}}{3}\right) {/tex}
    {tex} =\pi\left(\frac{4 {R}^2}{9}\right)\left(\frac{{H}}{3}\right) {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{4}{9}\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi {R}^2 {H}\right) {/tex}
    The expression for volume of cone,
    {tex} {V}_{{c}}=\frac{1}{3} \pi {R}^2 {H}{/tex}
    Substitute the volume of cone in the expression of maximum volume of cylinder.
    {tex} \therefore {V}_{\max }=\frac{4}{9} {~V}_{{c}}{/tex}
    Therefore, the greatest volume of cylinder that can be inscribed in cone has {tex}4 / 9{/tex} volume of cone. That have proved.

Show that the height of the right circular cylinder of greatest volume which can be inscribed in a right circular cone of height h and radius r is one-third of the height of the cone, and the greatest volume of the cylinder is {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} times the volume of the cone. Solution: The below figure represents the cylinder inscribed in cone, Schematic diagram In a cone, radius {tex}={R}{/tex} , height {tex}={H}{/tex} Semi vertical angle {tex}=\theta{/tex} In a cylinder, radius= {tex}r{/tex} , height=h The expression of similar triangle, {tex}\triangle {ACE} \sim \triangle {FCD}{/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac{{CE}}{{EA}}=\frac{{CD}}{{DF}}{/tex} Substitute the given values, {tex} \therefore \frac{H}{R}=\frac{H-h}{r} {/tex} {tex} \therefore h=H-\frac{H}{R} \gamma {/tex} The expression of volume of cylinder, {tex} {V}=\pi {r}^2 {~h}{/tex} Substitute the given value of height of cylinder, {tex} V=\pi r^2\left(H-\frac{H}{R} r\right) {/tex} {tex} \therefore V=\pi r^2 H-\pi r^3 \frac{H}{R} {/tex} Differentiate the above expression with respect to radius r and equate it to zero, {tex} \therefore \frac{{d}}{{dr}}\left(\pi r^2 H-\pi r^3 \frac{H}{R}\right)=0 {/tex} {tex} \therefore 2 \pi r H-3 \pi r^2 \frac{H}{R}=0 {/tex} {tex} \left.\therefore r=\frac{2 R}{3} \text { (because radius }>0\right) {/tex} Second differentiate at the given value of radius is negative. So, it gives maxima. Substitute the value of radius in the height of cylinder. {tex} \therefore h=H-\frac{H}{R}\left(\frac{2 R}{3}\right) {/tex} {tex} \therefore h=\frac{H}{3} {/tex} Substitute the calculated value of radius and height in the expression of volume of cylinder. {tex} \therefore {V}_{\max }=\pi\left(\frac{2 {R}}{3}\right)^2\left(\frac{{H}}{3}\right) {/tex} {tex} =\pi\left(\frac{4 {R}^2}{9}\right)\left(\frac{{H}}{3}\right) {/tex} {tex} =\frac{4}{9}\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi {R}^2 {H}\right) {/tex} The expression for volume of cone, {tex} {V}_{{c}}=\frac{1}{3} \pi {R}^2 {H}{/tex} Substitute the volume of cone in the expression of maximum volume of cylinder. {tex} \therefore {V}_{\max }=\frac{4}{9} {~V}_{{c}}{/tex} Therefore, the greatest volume of cylinder that can be inscribed in cone has {tex}4 / 9{/tex} volume of cone.
